channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israeli strike on mosque kills 19 as it bombards northern Gaza and southern Beirut
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Beirut in its widening war on Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lane Thomas hits 3-run homer, Cleveland's bullpen torments Tigers as Guardians win ALDS opener 7-0
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Lane Thomas hit a three-run homer in a five-run outburst before Detroit got an out, and the Cleveland Guardians unleashed their lights-out bullpen to complete a four-hitter in a 7-0 win over the Tigers in an AL Division Series opener on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states. Helene came ashore Sept.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vietnam veteran says he’s ‘honored’ to give Trump his Purple Heart after assassination attempt: ‘Small token’
item.description: Former President Trump on Friday met a Vietnam veteran who gave him his Purple Heart after being inspired by Trump's fortitude after an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man poses as woman on dating app to rob at gunpoint in St. Louis
item.description: A St. Louis County man allegedly posed as a woman on dating apps to lure other men into meetups, only to rob them at gunpoint, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: County program in Michigan offers savings for veterans
item.description: Kalamazoo County veterans can now get extra savings from area businesses thanks to a new rewards program launched by the Office of County Clerk and Register of Deeds this week.
item.dc:creator: Josh Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child entrepreneur opens pizza shop with Caribbean twist in New York City
item.description: A young entrepreneur is bringing even more Caribbean flavor to New York City, opening a pizza shop with a Caribbean twist at 11 years old.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, ABC 4

item.title: Ardent Trump supporters weigh in on safety at historic Pennsylvania rally
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service has "heightened" the security at the Trump rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, with rally goers saying that they felt safe attending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What are New York's squatter laws? Legal expert shares thoughts on what more can be done to protect landlords
item.description: New York's state government has taken action to improve squatter situations, especially in the city, but a legal expert thinks more can be done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: False and misleading claims about Hurricane Helene are spreading about the storm and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court’s election-season term that begins Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, WGN 9

item.title: Utah State doctoral student looks to help her hometown struck by Hurricane Helene
item.description: One Utah State University student is trying to look out for her and her neighbors in North Carolina. Debris from homes and businesses after Hurricane Helene is still being cleaned up almost a week after landfall.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia mom, newborn twins killed sheltering from Hurricane Helene; twins are likely storm's youngest victims
item.description: Two Georgia newborn twins became the youngest known victims of Hurricane Helene when they died last week along with their mother, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nigerian track star Oba Femi shines as NXT superstar
item.description: Femi will defend his title Tuesday night on the CW Network. It's a show that also features WWE legend Randy Orton.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, Fox 5

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: The former first lady said Wednesday that she thinks Americans need to listen to one another, even if they hold different beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: The fate of Nibi the beaver lands in court as rescuers try to stop her release into the wild
item.description: Whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi gets to stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild as winter approaches in Massachusetts has ended up in court — and caused such an uproar that even the governor has weighed in.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chances increase for tropical development in the Gulf
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to strengthen bringing swells to the U.S. east coast on Sunday, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
